rows.notes: Page year heading clearly written at top centre of the page, no monetary columns present for this item.
Clear, large numerals aligned with the three right-hand ruling lines (360/12/7 style). All three columns are unambiguous and within valid ranges.

Numerals read as '5 5 0'. Vertical alignment suggests these occupy the £, s, d columns respectively. Ink is reasonably clear though the first digit is small; chosen interpretation (£5 5s 0d) fits visible alignment. Minor ambiguity whether the first '5' could be a shilling (i.e. 0£ 5s 5d), but spacing favors pounds.

rows.description: Rowell's Stipend for care of the Clock to Mids. 1825
rows.notes: Clear trio of digits '3 3 0' aligned under the three ruling lines; reads as £3 3s 0d. Text of description legible; numbers well aligned and valid.
Digits '2 0 0' visible and well aligned; interpreted as £2 0s 0d. Column boundaries clear and values valid.

Only two digits are clearly visible near the right-hand columns, read as '12 0' in the s and d columns (i.e. 0£ 12s 0d). The leftmost (pounds) column is blank for this row. Slight uncertainty because the left edge of the middle column is faint; chosen interpretation fits typical small payment for a watchman and column alignment.

Visible numerals read as '17 0' located in the shillings and pence area; interpreted as 0£ 17s 0d. There is moderate ambiguity whether the first digit is a '1' in the pounds column making it £1 7s 0d, but the spacing relative to the ruling lines favors 17 shillings in the s column.

rows.description: Land Tax for the Stable yard one yr. & Day. 1825
rows.notes: Digits appear as '1 7 6' and align with the £, s, d columns. The '6' in the pence is clear and looks like a 6 rather than a blemish. All values within valid ranges; minor ink faintness on the '1'.

rows.description: Easter offerings to St. Michael's Parish
rows.notes: Reads clearly as '1 1 0' aligned under the three columns; interpretation £1 1s 0d is straightforward and matches typical offering amounts.
Clear numeric '11 9 6' in the £, s, d columns. The printed calculation '17 at 13/6' matches the resulting amount of £11 9s 6d, confirming accuracy and alignment.

rows.description: Arch Deacon's Procurations for Kill. Rectory 2 prs 3
rows.notes: Numerals '1 4 0' clearly visible and aligned. Description contains a small marginal note '2 prs 3' which is included in the description text exactly as it appears. Numbers are unambiguous and valid.
Final underlined total at lower right of the column block, very clear digits and alignment. Validates column positions used above.
